$comment: D-CSUBSET-CHAR-ALU-INTEGER-PROMOTION / D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS (c71, 2026-07-01): a SUB-INT (`char`/`signed char`) operand of a truth-value condition, of ++/--, OR of a logical `!` must be integer-PROMOTED to `int` (C 6.3.1.1 / 6.5.3.3p5) BEFORE the ICmp/Sub, else the op is Char/I8-width and walls at the LIR sub-native ALU gap ('integer TypeKind ordinal 15 has no native-width ALU forms'). c70 cleared sqlite's large VDBE switch, so sqlite3.c compiled end-to-end and hit this x902 (a text library scans/steps `char` everywhere). ROOT CAUSE: THREE HIR-lowering sites built the op directly, bypassing the promotion the ordinary binary combine site already does: (A) coerceCondition minted a SAME-TYPE (Char) zero -> Ne(char,char) for a bare-char truth test (`while(*z)`,`if(c)`); (B) incDecArithValue -> incDecArithType(Char)=Char (only Enum was remapped) -> Add/Sub at opType=Char for `c++`/`c--`; (A') combineUnaryOp's logical `!` -> a Char-typed ICmpEq(operand,0) (the pervasive `if(!*z)` scan idiom; found by c71's independent audit). FIX: all reuse the existing `integerPromotedType`+`coerce` chokepoint (as the array-index path does) -> Char/signed->SExt, unsigned->ZExt via mapCast; the inc/dec trailing coerce narrows the i32 result back (Trunc), preserving 8-bit wraparound EXACTLY; `!` keeps its Bool result (Bool operand excluded — its ICmpEq form is already native). Corpus = ONE function of SEQUENTIAL loops (stays under the -O inline register-pressure cliff): while(*z)'hello'=5 + count 'a' in 'banana'=3 + plain-char c++ x10=10 + signed-char 126->127->-128 wrap=1 + if((signed char)0x80)=10 + (hi<0 SExt)=7 + !*'' (empty, !0=1)=6 + !5 (=0, false arm)=0 => 42. Every observable is INDEPENDENT of plain-char signedness (DSS types char signed uniformly; gcc uses unsigned char on arm64) so it matches gcc on all 4 legs; the two signedness assertions use `signed char` explicitly. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the promotions -> error[L_UnsupportedLoweringForOpcode] 'ICmp/Sub operand: integer TypeKind ordinal 15 (Char) / 1 (signed char) ...' (a compile failure, not 42). gcc/clang compile it and exit 42. SIBLINGS NOT fixed here (their own lowering sites): a `switch(char)` discriminant (deferred D-CSUBSET-NARROW-SWITCH-DISCRIMINANT-CMP) and unary `-c`/`~c` (Neg/BitNot on char — a result-type change; handle if a re-probe hits them).
